_warning: HAND-AUTHORED. A sync script that writes this file is a bug. No upstream source has EVENTS as entities — the duels, wars, deaths and declarations exist only inside chapter prose — so every row here is hand-typed. verified:false means Shawn has not confirmed the chapters against the manga yet.
_why_this_file_exists: The atlas charts WHERE things are as of a chapter; this file charts WHAT HAPPENED there. api-onepiece.com and the wiki have arcs and debuts, but no on-page event with a chapter stamp and a place. The Zoro–Mihawk duel, the fall of Arlong Park, the war at Marineford — these are the beats the whole map exists to locate, and nobody ships them as data.
_semantics: occurred_chapter is when the READER sees the event on the page — the fruit_reveals rule, not in-world truth. Roger's execution happened decades before chapter 1 in-world; the reader watches it in the chapter 1 prologue, so occurred_chapter is 1. through_chapter (nullable) closes a multi-chapter beat; the event is chapter-gated on occurred_chapter alone. Position: island_slug resolves via canon/islands.coords.json, OR an explicit lng/lat for open-sea events (the Baratie is a floating restaurant with no island row — same convention as voyage_legs and presence windows). An event must never occur on an island the reader has not charted: normalize.py refuses occurred_chapter < the island's debut_chapter.
_kinds: duel = a formal one-on-one. battle = a fight that decides an arc. war = a multi-front conflict. declaration = words that change the world on the page. death = an on-page death. execution = a public execution (attempted or carried out). oath = a promise the story keeps returning to. escape = a getaway that closes a beat. departure = a crossing or setting-out that moves the voyage itself.
_significance: 1 = arc beat. 2 = saga-defining. 3 = era-defining (the map should whisper about it).
_participants: Character slugs from data/canon.json characters[], validated by normalize.py. role is a short free label ('victor', 'defeated', 'duelist', 'executed'). Crews and armies are named in prose via outcome, not as rows — a participant here is a person the reader watched.
_before_any_content_ships: Every row is verified:false. Shawn must confirm every occurred_chapter against the manga and flip verified to true. Until then the UI renders these as unverified.

events.name: The Straw Hat Promise
events.outcome: Shanks leaves his straw hat with Luffy — to be returned when Luffy becomes a great pirate. The whole voyage is this promise being kept.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Chapter 1 (Romance Dawn): Shanks gives Luffy the straw hat as he departs Foosha Village. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: The Execution of Gol D. Roger
events.outcome: The Pirate King dies at Loguetown — and his last words about the One Piece ignite the Great Pirate Era. In-world this is decades before the story; the reader watches it in the chapter 1 prologue, so it is charted from chapter 1.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Chapter 1 prologue: Roger's execution at Loguetown and the words that launch the era. occurred_chapter follows the reader-time rule. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: The Fall of Axe-Hand Morgan
events.outcome: Luffy and Zoro topple the tyrant of the 153rd Marine Branch — the crew's first fight as a crew.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Shells Town: Morgan is beaten and Zoro sails with Luffy (~ch. 6). The exact defeat chapter is a reading of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Buggy Blasted Away at Orange Town
events.outcome: Nami ties Buggy's scattered parts together and Luffy sends the rest flying — Orange Town is free, and the map's longest-running grudge begins.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Orange Town: Luffy defeats Buggy with Nami's help (~ch. 20). The exact chapter is a reading of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Kuro of a Hundred Plans Falls
events.outcome: The butler's three-year plan dies on the beach; Syrup Village never learns it was ever in danger, because Usopp the liar tells them nothing happened.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Syrup Village: Luffy defeats Captain Kuro (~ch. 39). The exact chapter is a reading of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Zoro vs. Mihawk — the Duel at the Baratie
events.outcome: The world's greatest swordsman opens Zoro's chest and spares his life for his ambition. Zoro's vow to Luffy — never to lose again until he takes the title — is the oath the whole swordsman's arc hangs on.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. The Baratie, open sea (~ch. 50–51): Mihawk defeats Zoro; the never-again vow follows. Explicit lng/lat: the Baratie is a floating restaurant with no island row; position matches the voyage's Baratie anchor. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Don Krieg Defeated at the Baratie
events.outcome: The self-styled strongest armada of East Blue breaks against one rubber pirate defending a floating restaurant; Sanji sails soon after.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. The Baratie: Luffy defeats Don Krieg (~ch. 66). Explicit lng/lat: the Baratie has no island row; position matches the voyage's Baratie anchor. The exact chapter is a reading of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: The Fall of Arlong Park
events.outcome: Luffy brings Arlong Park down on its owner's head. Eight years of Cocoyasi's tribute end, and Nami joins the crew for real — the hat on her head while she cried is the hinge of East Blue.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Conomi Islands: Luffy defeats Arlong and the Park collapses (~ch. 93). The exact chapter is a reading of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: The Escape from the Execution Platform
events.outcome: On the platform where Roger died, Buggy's blade falls — and lightning falls first. Luffy grins where the Pirate King grinned, and Smoker decides this one is his to chase.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Loguetown: the execution attempt and the lightning strike (~ch. 99). The exact chapter is a reading of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

events.name: Into the Grand Line
events.outcome: The barrel oath in the storm, and the Going Merry rides Reverse Mountain up and over — East Blue ends, the Grand Line begins.
events.source_ref: Hand-authored. Reverse Mountain (~ch. 100–101): the crew's declarations over the barrel and the ascent into the Grand Line. Charted at ch. 101 with the island's debut. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.
